message:
feat(fts5,core,func): tokenizer zero-copy for ASCII tokens, join row emission refactor, and datetime seconds sign rejection

Three independent correctness and performance improvements:

1. FTS5 tokenizer zero-copy (fsqlite-ext-fts5/src/lib.rs):
   Unicode61Tokenizer and AsciiTokenizer now track a borrowed_ascii_token
   flag. When a token is all-lowercase ASCII or digits, the tokenizer
   emits a borrowed &text[start..end] slice directly instead of building
   a String via char::to_lowercase(). Falls back to the owned path as
   soon as any uppercase or non-ASCII character appears. Eliminates
   per-token String allocation for the dominant case in typical FTS5
   indexing workloads.

2. Connection join row emission refactor (fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs):
   Extracts emit_prepared_simple_join_rows_raw() from the existing
   emit_prepared_simple_join_rows() — the raw variant emits full joined
   rows without projection, which the FTS5 INSERT INTO...SELECT path
   uses with a direct_projection fast path to avoid the generic
   build_live_vtab_insert_row overhead per row.

3. DateTime seconds sign rejection (fsqlite-func/src/datetime.rs):
   Extends the signed-time-field rejection to the seconds component.
   C SQLite's computeHMS rejects "12:30:+45" but Rust's f64::from_str
   accepts leading signs. Adds test cases for signed seconds fields
   including "12:30:+45" and "12:30:+45.123".

Also updates beads issue tracker state (closes bd-ooc2c: Track M
epoch-based reclamation verified and landed).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
